title: When Ocean Floor Became Coffee Floor
subtitle: How the Gulf's Greatest Disaster Led to America's Most Expensive Burrito

ripples.headline: Deepwater Horizon explosion kills 11 workers, triggers massive oil spill in Gulf of Mexico
Gulf fishing industry devastated as oil contamination spreads across 68,000 square miles
Seafood prices surge nationwide as Gulf supply chain collapses, shrimp costs triple
Restaurant chains pivot to Pacific imports, driving up West Coast fishing demand by 40%
California avocado farmers capitalize on increased restaurant demand, expand operations rapidly
Avocado shortage hits peak season as demand outstrips supply, wholesale prices jump 300%
Local taco shop owner Mike Rodriguez forced to charge $18 for burrito due to avocado costs

symphony_arc.opening: An explosion in the depths of the Gulf sets off waves that would eventually crash into a San Diego taco stand
symphony_arc.development: Environmental catastrophe cascades through seafood markets, reshaping America's dining landscape one shortage at a time
symphony_arc.climax: As Gulf waters burn, West Coast farms scramble to feed a nation's suddenly redirected appetite
symphony_arc.resolution: Two years later, Mike Rodriguez apologetically explains to a customer why their Tuesday burrito now costs more than a steak dinner

choices: How much would you pay for guac?
Was this burrito worth the environmental cost?
Should Mike switch to cheaper ingredients?
